notes: Gunstar Heroes is a run-and-gun platformer known for technical achievement on Genesis hardware.
DEFINING MECHANIC: Weapon combination system — 4 base weapons combined in pairs = 14 total weapon combinations.
Two movement types selectable at start: 'Free Shot' (can shoot in 8 directions) or 'Fixed Shot' (faster movement, locks shoot direction).
Slide/aerial slide provides fast horizontal movement.
Can throw and slam enemies — melee-based crowd control.
Two-player co-op. One of the earliest games to demonstrate what Treasure would become known for: technical showmanship.

movement_types._note: Player selects movement type before starting — not changeable during game.

free_shot.description: Can aim and shoot in 8 directions independently of movement.
free_shot.notes: More versatile combat but slower movement.

fixed_shot.description: Shooting direction is locked to movement direction. Enables faster horizontal movement speed.
fixed_shot.notes: Preferred for speedruns — the faster movement compensates for less flexible aiming.

walk_speed_approx.notes: Approximate. Fixed Shot mode is faster. VERIFY.

slide.description: Ground slide for quick horizontal movement and avoiding projectiles.
slide.notes: Slide can be performed in both ground and air (aerial slide).

aerial_slide.description: Press slide in the air for a fast horizontal aerial dash.
aerial_slide.notes: Aerial slide is a key traversal tool — allows fast horizontal movement across gaps.

weapon_system._note: The weapon combination system is the core design innovation of Gunstar Heroes.

base_weapons.force: Unlimited bouncing bullets (as many as you can fire)

combination_system.description: Two weapons are combined to create a new weapon type. Each combination creates a unique weapon with properties from both parents.

notable_combinations.force_plus_force: The strongest crowd-clearing weapon — infinite bouncing projectiles fill the screen
notable_combinations.fire_plus_chaser: Homing fire — very effective against bosses

melee_combat.description: Gunstar Heroes features melee attacks in addition to ranged weapons.
melee_combat.grab: Press the melee button near an enemy to grab them
melee_combat.throw: After grabbing, throw the enemy as a projectile — damages other enemies on contact
melee_combat.slam: Grab + slam into the ground — area damage
melee_combat.notes: The grab-and-throw mechanic makes enemies into weapons. This creates a risk-reward: get close (vulnerable) for melee advantage.

rolling.description: Characters can roll on the ground for evasion and collision avoidance.
rolling.notes: Rolling can be used to avoid projectiles.

design_notes_for_giants_drink: The weapon combination system turns item collection into a creative act. Players don't just find 'better weapons' — they experiment with combinations to discover interactions. 14 weapons from 4 components is elegant combinatorial design.

Free Shot vs Fixed Shot mode selection creates two different games from the same content. Free Shot = combat precision; Fixed Shot = movement speed. This is a difficulty/style choice that lets players self-select their challenge.

The grab-and-throw mechanic makes every enemy a potential weapon. This creates a positional economy: enemies you kill normally vs. enemies you use as projectiles against other enemies. Using the map dynamically.

Gunstar Heroes' technical achievement (sprites with rotation/scaling, large boss sprites, smooth frame rate) was a deliberate flex of Genesis hardware capabilities. The game is designed to show off what the hardware can do — and the design choices support that showmanship.

Two-player co-op changes the game significantly — two players can cover different threat sources, use grab-and-throw cooperatively, and split weapon combination strategies. The co-op is meaningful, not just additive.
